document_map.document_title: EXHIBIT D — DUTY OF CARE & TRAVEL RISK MANAGEMENT SERVICES
document_map.document_purpose_summary: This Exhibit defines the Duty of Care services Direct Travel will provide to support Client's travel risk management program, including service classifications, roles and responsibilities, data handling requirements, and limitations applicable to such services.

sections.section_purpose: Establishes document purpose and legal framework
sections.section_summary: This section defines the purpose of the exhibit as establishing duty of care services, regulatory background, and standard of care requirements for travel risk management.
sections.beginning_text: This Exhibit defines (a) the Duty of Care services Direct Travel will provide to support Client's travel risk management ("TRM") program, (b) Severity Level classifications and corresponding service level objectives, (c) roles and responsibilities of each party, (d) data handling and privacy requirements, (e) escalation and crisis communication protocols, and (f) limitations applicable to such services.

sections.section_summary: This section provides comprehensive definitions for technical terms and concepts used throughout the exhibit, including booking channels, incident classifications, and service components.
sections.beginning_text: "Approved Channels" means bookings made through (a) Client's designated online booking tool(s) as provided by Direct Travel, (b) Direct Travel agent-assisted channels (phone, email, online chat), and (c) any other booking channels expressly approved in writing by Client and integrated into the TMC's itinerary data feed.

sections.section_name: SCOPE OF DUTY OF CARE SERVICES
sections.section_purpose: Details specific service offerings and capabilities
sections.section_summary: This section comprehensively describes the duty of care services Direct Travel will provide, including traveler locate capabilities, risk monitoring, pre-trip services, incident response, and post-incident reporting.
sections.beginning_text: Direct Travel will capture and maintain itinerary records for all travel booked through Approved Channels, including air, hotel, rail, car rental, and ground transportation segments, regardless of airline distribution channel.

sections.section_purpose: Defines differentiated service levels by traveler type
sections.section_summary: This section establishes that differentiated service levels for specific traveler populations and booking approval workflows will be defined in the Statement of Work.
sections.beginning_text: Where Client requires differentiated service levels for specific Traveler populations (e.g., VIP/executive travelers, high-risk destination travelers, group travel), such categories and the corresponding service enhancements will be defined in the SOW.

sections.section_name: SEVERITY LEVELS AND SERVICE LEVEL OBJECTIVES
sections.section_purpose: Establishes incident classification and response standards
sections.section_summary: This section defines the four-level severity classification system for incidents and establishes corresponding service level objectives for response times and communication cadence.
sections.beginning_text: Severity determination is made by Direct Travel using commercially reasonable judgment informed by the Risk Intelligence Platform. Client may request a change to classification at any time; Direct Travel will consider such requests promptly and document the outcome.

sections.section_purpose: Defines communication protocols and escalation procedures
sections.section_summary: This section establishes client escalation roster requirements, non-responsive traveler escalation procedures, and crisis bridge activation protocols for Level 4 incidents.
sections.beginning_text: Client shall provide Direct Travel with a 24/7 escalation roster containing, at minimum: Primary Travel Program Owner, Alternate Travel Program Contact, After-Hours Duty Contact, Corporate Security (if applicable), Human Resources Duty Contact (if applicable), and Senior Leadership Contact.

sections.section_purpose: Establishes data protection and privacy requirements
sections.section_summary: This section defines data processing roles, lawful basis for processing traveler data, minimum required profile data, data sharing protocols during incidents, and cross-border transfer requirements.
sections.beginning_text: Direct Travel will process Traveler Data only to provide services under the Agreement and as otherwise permitted by the Agreement's data protection terms. Where required by applicable law, a Data Processing Agreement ("DPA") is incorporated by reference or attached as a separate exhibit to the Agreement.

sections.section_summary: This section establishes the client's obligations including maintaining corporate travel policy, providing escalation roster, ensuring traveler data accuracy, and authorizing extraordinary measures during incidents.
sections.beginning_text: Establish, maintain, and communicate a corporate travel policy that includes duty of care provisions, requires booking through Approved Channels, and defines approval requirements for high-risk destinations.

sections.section_summary: This section explains the visibility gap created by off-channel bookings, describes mitigation measures available, and establishes client acknowledgment of service limitations for unmanaged travel.
sections.beginning_text: Industry data indicates that approximately 30% of corporate travel bookings are made outside managed travel programs. Direct Travel can only provide Duty of Care Services for travel that is visible in its systems.

sections.section_name: LIMITATIONS, DISCLAIMERS, AND ALLOCATION OF RISK
sections.section_purpose: Establishes service limitations and liability boundaries
sections.section_summary: This section provides comprehensive disclaimers regarding safety guarantees, service dependencies, professional advice limitations, supplier actions, and liability allocation for duty of care services.
sections.beginning_text: Direct Travel does not and cannot guarantee Traveler safety. The Duty of Care Services are designed to support Client's TRM program through information, communication, and coordination.

sections.section_purpose: Defines fee structure and cost allocation
sections.section_summary: This section outlines the fee structure for duty of care services and identifies pass-through costs that are borne by the client, including specialist provider fees and emergency transportation costs.
sections.beginning_text: Fees for Duty of Care Services are specified in the SOW and may include: a per-Traveler or per-trip fee component; a platform access fee for risk intelligence dashboard and reporting; incident response fees (if any, based on Severity Level and effort); and Specialist Provider access fees.

sections.section_purpose: Establishes program review and improvement processes
sections.section_summary: This section defines quarterly business review requirements, post-incident review procedures, notification obligations for material changes, and amendment processes for continuous program improvement.
sections.beginning_text: The parties will conduct quarterly business reviews (or at the cadence specified in the SOW) that include duty of care program metrics, including: Incident counts by Severity Level and geography; SLO performance (response times, outreach completion rates); Traveler welfare check response rates; Off-Channel booking visibility gap analysis; and program improvement recommendations.

sections.section_purpose: Defines term duration and surviving provisions
sections.section_summary: This section establishes that the exhibit is coterminous with the main agreement and identifies which provisions survive termination or expiration.
sections.beginning_text: This Exhibit is coterminous with the Agreement unless earlier terminated in accordance with the Agreement's termination provisions.

sections.section_summary: This section defines the order of precedence among the Agreement, SOW, and this Exhibit in the event of conflicting provisions.
sections.beginning_text: In the event of conflict between this Exhibit and other components of the Agreement: (1) the Agreement (master terms), (2) the SOW, then (3) this Exhibit, unless the SOW expressly states otherwise with specific reference to the provision being overridden.

sections.section_purpose: Provides signature block for agreement execution
sections.section_summary: This section provides the signature block for both Direct Travel, Inc. and Client to execute the exhibit.
This appendix provides detailed tables of configurable parameters for outreach, coverage model, integrations, pre-trip services, and reporting that should be defined in the SOW.
sections.beginning_text: The following parameters are configurable per Client and should be defined in the SOW:

sections.section_summary: This appendix provides standardized message templates for different severity levels of travel alerts and welfare checks, including SMS, email, and voice script formats.
sections.beginning_text: **Subject:** Travel Advisory — [EVENT TYPE] in [CITY/REGION] A [EVENT TYPE] has been reported in/near [CITY/REGION]. Based on current assessment, this event is classified as **Level 1 — Advisory** and is not expected to directly impact your travel plans.

sections.section_name: APPENDIX C — INCIDENT ACTIVITY LOG TEMPLATE
sections.section_summary: This appendix specifies the required fields for incident activity logs that Direct Travel will maintain for Level 3 and Level 4 incidents.
sections.beginning_text: For Level 3 and Level 4 Incidents, Direct Travel will maintain an incident activity log containing the following fields:

global_entity_pre_registration.brief_description: The contracting organization entering into the Travel Services Agreement with Direct Travel, referenced throughout as "Client"
The travel management company providing duty of care services, also referred to as "Direct Travel" or "TMC", first appears in document header
The master Travel Services Agreement between Client and Direct Travel into which this exhibit is incorporated, first appears in document header
Statement of Work, also referenced as "Statement of Work", contains specific service configurations and parameters, first appears in document header
A Client employee or authorized person traveling on Client business, defined in section 2.12
An event that may reasonably impact Traveler safety, security, health, or ability to continue travel, defined in section 2.7
Third-party risk management platform designated by Direct Travel, currently Crisis24 Horizon, defined in section 2.6
Bookings made through Client's designated online booking tools, Direct Travel agent-assisted channels, or other expressly approved channels, defined in section 2.1
Travel booking made outside of Approved Channels, including direct bookings on consumer sites, defined in section 2.8
Level 1 through Level 4 classification system for incidents, defined in section 2.10 and detailed in section 5
Real-time conference line and/or digital collaboration channel established during Level 4 Incidents, defined in section 2.4
Third-party provider of security, medical, insurance, evacuation, or other specialized risk management services, defined in section 2.11
Personal data related to Travelers processed by Direct Travel in connection with providing services, defined in section 2.13
The services described in Section 3 for supporting Client's travel risk management program, defined in section 2.5
Hours defined in the SOW during which Client's primary service team is staffed, including applicable time zones, defined in section 2.3

pre_registration_scope_note: This list is a known-incomplete seed whose sole purpose is cross-section entity naming coordination. It does not represent all entities in the document. Stage 2 extractors are required to discover and register additional entities not present in this list through independent analysis of section text. The absence of an entity from this list carries no implication about its importance or relevance to the ontology graph.

cross_section_dependencies.dependency_description: Section 2 defines key terms like Approved Channels, Incident, Risk Intelligence Platform, and Traveler that are used extensively throughout the service descriptions in Section 3.
Section 5 defines the Severity Level classifications that determine which services in Section 3 are triggered and at what response levels.
Section 7 establishes data processing requirements and limitations that constrain how the services described in Section 3 can collect, use, and share traveler data.

Section 8 defines Client obligations that are prerequisites for the effective delivery of the services described in Section 3, including maintaining traveler data accuracy and providing escalation contacts.
Section 9 explains significant limitations to the services in Section 3 by identifying that Off-Channel Bookings create visibility gaps that prevent service delivery.
Section 10 provides comprehensive disclaimers and limitations that qualify all services described in Section 3, establishing boundaries on Direct Travel's responsibilities and liabilities.
Section 2 defines the Incident term that is classified using the Severity Levels established in Section 5.
Section 5 defines the Severity Levels that trigger different escalation and communication protocols detailed in Section 6.
Section 2 defines Traveler Data and related terms that are the subject of the data protection requirements established in Section 7.
Appendix A references the SOW defined in Section 2 as the location where the configurable parameters should be specified.
Appendix B provides message templates that correspond to the Severity Level classifications defined in Section 5.
Appendix C specifies incident logging requirements for Level 3 and Level 4 incidents as defined by the Severity Level classifications in Section 5.
